title: rudbeckia occidentalis var. alpicola
intro: Rudbeckia occidentalis var. alpicola, commonly known as the alpine coneflower, is a perennial plant species that belongs to the daisy family (Asteraceae). The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which have been attributed to its high concentration of flavonoids and sesquiterpenes. The health benefits of Rudbeckia occidentalis var. alpicola include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve wound healing, and exhibit antioxidant activity, making it a popular ingredient in herbal remedies for skin and respiratory issues. The plant can be prepared through various methods, including infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat a range of health conditions, from skin irritations to respiratory infections.
